metadata.title: Cross-border settlement rails — the dollar-bypass stack (CIPS, Project mBridge, e-CNY, BRICS Pay) vs the dollar-extension rail (USD stablecoins/SWIFT), and the reality of de-dollarization
metadata.frame: The macro counterpart to the Asia-stablecoin map: two competing futures for cross-border money. (1) A DOLLAR-BYPASS stack - China's CIPS messaging/settlement network, the multi-CBDC Project mBridge (live 2025, bypassing SWIFT), the e-CNY, and BRICS Pay - built partly to insulate trade from US sanctions. (2) A DOLLAR-EXTENSION rail - USD stablecoins (USDC/RLUSD/USDT) on public chains plus SWIFT, which deepen dollar usage and the Treasury bid. Evidence-graded, with an explicit reality-check: de-dollarization is real at the MARGINS (reserves, bilateral trade) but the dollar's transactional dominance is intact or rising. The US GENIUS-Act stablecoin push reads partly as a counter-move to defend dollar primacy against the bypass stack.
metadata.relevance: Interleaves [[macro-stablecoin-treasury-rail]] (USD-stablecoin dollar-extension), [[spec-market-plumbing-control]] (SWIFT/DTCC), [[fin-gulf-sovereign-ai-capital]] (UAE/Saudi as mBridge members), [[geopolitics-russia-energy-arctic]] (Russia's sanctions-driven bypass), [[macro-us-fiscal-trap]] (dollar/Treasury stakes), and [[spec-asia-crypto-payments]] (the retail/payments layer).
metadata.sources: BIS Innovation Hub (mBridge), HKMA, Reuters (BIS step-back), CSIS / Statrys / FXC Intelligence / Wikipedia (CIPS), Modern Diplomacy / watcher.guru (BRICS), BIS Triennial FX Survey 2025 (dollar share). Full URLs in the rendered .md.

swift_incumbent.summary: SWIFT is the incumbent messaging layer - and its use as a sanctions weapon is exactly what spurred the challengers.

points.detail: SWIFT connects 11,500+ institutions and remains the dominant cross-border messaging standard.
Cutting Russian banks off SWIFT (2022) demonstrated the dollar/SWIFT system's coercive power - the explicit motive for adversaries and even some partners to build alternatives.
SWIFT signed an MOU to work alongside CIPS - treating it as a partner to interoperate with rather than a pure rival.

cips_china_network.summary: China's Cross-Border Interbank Payment System is the fastest-growing dollar-alternative settlement network - large in flow, still small in reach.

points.detail: CIPS processed ~RMB 175T (~$24.5T) in 2024, ~38% CAGR 2016-2024; by end-2025, 193 direct + 1,573 indirect participants across 124 countries.
CIPS briefly surpassed SWIFT's single-day throughput, processing ~$1.76T on 16 Apr 2025 - a symbolic milestone, though SWIFT's institution count (11,500+) still dwarfs CIPS's reach.
CIPS lets China and partners (notably Russia) settle trade outside dollar/SWIFT exposure - the practical core of de-dollarization at the transaction layer.

mbridge_multi_cbdc.summary: The most consequential dollar-bypass experiment: a live multi-CBDC platform that settles cross-border without SWIFT - and is now majority Gulf-and-China-run.

points.detail: Founding/core central banks: China (PBoC), Hong Kong (HKMA), Thailand, UAE (CBUAE); Saudi Arabia (SAMA) joined as a full participant Jun 2024. Observer central banks across SE Asia, the Middle East, and Africa.

mBridge went live in 2025 as the first commercial cross-border CBDC settlement system, enabling near-instant settlement that bypasses SWIFT; it has processed ~RMB 387B (~$55B), ~95% of it in DIGITAL YUAN.

The BIS (which incubated mBridge via its HK hub) reportedly moved to step back from the project (late 2024), leaving China and Gulf states to steward a dollar-bypass rail - a notable governance shift.
UAE + Saudi membership ties mBridge directly to the project's Gulf-sovereign-capital thread - the same states deploying AI capital are building the dollar-bypass settlement layer.

brics_dedollar.summary: A broader BRICS de-dollarization agenda - real in bilateral trade and reserves, aspirational in a common currency.

points.detail: A BRICS cross-border payments system demoed late 2024, in early pilot - intended to link members' national systems outside the dollar.
Russia reported ~90% of its trade within the bloc now settles in national currencies (yuan-heavy) - sanctions-forced de-dollarization that is genuine at the trade layer.
A 'BRICS currency' remains rhetoric, not reality; gold accumulation by member central banks is the more concrete reserve-diversification move.

reality_check.summary: The essential corrective: de-dollarization is real at the margins but the dollar's transactional dominance is intact - both the hype and the dismissal mislead.

points.detail: The BIS 2025 Triennial Survey found the USD on one side of 89.2% of all FX transactions (April 2025), UP from 88.4% in 2022 - no transactional displacement.
The dollar's share of global reserves fell from ~73% (2001) to ~54% (2025) - a real, slow diversification (into other currencies and gold), not collapse.

Bilateral sanctioned-trade settlement (Russia-China), reserve diversification, and oil-trade pricing experiments - margins, not the core. Structural barriers (capital controls, RMB non-convertibility, trust, network effects) keep the dollar dominant.

the_two_rails_contest.summary: The strategic frame: a private dollar-extension rail vs a state dollar-bypass rail - and US policy is now consciously playing the first to defend the dollar.

points.detail: USD stablecoins (USDC/RLUSD/USDT) on public chains push dollar usage and Treasury demand globally ([[macro-stablecoin-treasury-rail]]); the GENIUS Act institutionalizes this. A market-led defense of dollar primacy.

CIPS + mBridge + e-CNY + BRICS Pay are state-led rails to settle outside the dollar/SWIFT - sovereignty and sanctions-resilience, but with the same state-control/programmability concerns the project flags for CBDCs.

If USD stablecoins win, the fiscal-trap Treasury bid gets a new buyer base; if the bypass stack scales, the US loses a coercive lever and a funding channel. Both are dual-use control rails, just for different states.

forced_vs_chosen_flows.summary: #60 - classify each bypass flow as SANCTIONS-FORCED (committed, irreversible near-term) vs STRATEGICALLY-CHOSEN (optionality/hedge, reversible, dual-track). The distinction is the whole strategic picture: forced flows are the bypass stack's DURABLE base; chosen flows are its GROWTH potential but are contingent and can evaporate. Flows/figures are fact; the forced/chosen classification is labeled analysis.

forced.flow: post-2022 SWIFT cutoff -> ~90% of intra-bloc trade in national currencies (yuan-heavy) via CIPS; energy sold in non-dollar
forced.why_forced: no access to dollar/SWIFT - the bypass is load-bearing for the economy, not a choice
forced.reversibility: irreversible while sanctioned; a captive, committed base

forced.flow: oil/trade settled outside the dollar (yuan, barter, crypto); Nobitex/IRGC-adjacent rails (cross-ref spec-onchain-threat-actor-addresses)
forced.why_forced: comprehensive sanctions leave no dollar option

chosen.flow: builds CIPS (~$24.5T 2024) + mBridge (~95% e-CNY) + e-CNY + BRICS Pay - yet remains deeply dollar-integrated (still holds Treasuries, trades in USD)
chosen.why_chosen: sovereignty + sanctions-INSURANCE + a coercive lever - optionality, NOT necessity; keeps BOTH rails
chosen.reversibility: reversible/dual-track - China can dial bypass usage up or down

chosen.flow: join mBridge (SAMA Jun-2024) AND hold dollars/Treasuries AND deploy AI capital in USD (fin-gulf-sovereign-ai-capital)
chosen.why_chosen: pure hedging - a foot on each rail; optionality maximization, no compulsion

chosen.flow: BRICS Pay pilot; common-currency rhetoric; reserve diversification into gold
chosen.why_chosen: aspirational optionality + reserve hedging, not transactional necessity

forced_vs_chosen_flows.the_strategic_asymmetry: The bypass stack's DURABLE demand = the FORCED flows (Russia/Iran) - small in global terms but committed and irreversible. Its GROWTH potential = the CHOSEN flows (China/Gulf/BRICS) - far larger but CONTINGENT: they persist only while building optionality stays cheap and using the bypass isn't costly. This is why the dollar's transactional dominance (89.2% of FX) coexists with a real bypass build-out: most of the build-out is reversible hedging, not committed exit.
forced_vs_chosen_flows.the_feedback_risk: The dangerous dynamic for the dollar: each sanctions action that FORCES a flow also incentivizes CHOSEN hedging by everyone watching - sanctions overuse converts reversible hedges into permanent exits (chosen -> forced). The US dollar-defense via USD stablecoins (the GENIUS-Act extension rail) is aimed precisely at the CHOSEN column: keep the dollar attractive/convenient enough that optionality-seekers don't migrate. So the contest is won or lost on the chosen flows, and lost fastest by sanctioning so broadly that 'chosen' becomes 'forced.'
forced_vs_chosen_flows.grade: flows + figures FACT (verification_log); the forced/chosen classification + the feedback-risk are labeled analysis

checks.result: Live 2025, bypasses SWIFT; members PBoC/HKMA/Thailand/UAE + Saudi (SAMA, Jun 2024); ~RMB 387B (~$55B) processed, ~95% e-CNY; BIS reported step-back late 2024 (BIS/HKMA/Reuters/Modern Diplomacy).
~RMB 175T (~$24.5T) 2024; 38% CAGR; 193 direct/1,573 indirect, 124 countries end-2025; ~$1.76T single-day 16 Apr 2025 briefly > SWIFT; SWIFT-CIPS MOU (CSIS/Statrys/FXC/Wikipedia).
BIS Triennial 2025: USD on 89.2% of FX (up from 88.4% 2022); reserve share ~73%->~54% (2001->2025).
BRICS Pay demoed late 2024 (early pilot); Russia ~90% intra-bloc trade in national currencies; common currency aspirational (Modern Diplomacy/watcher.guru).

non_claims: It is NOT asserted that the dollar is being displaced - transactional dominance is intact (89.2% of FX); de-dollarization is real only at the margins (reserves, sanctioned bilateral trade).
It is NOT asserted that mBridge/CIPS have achieved scale parity with SWIFT - they remain far smaller in reach.
Sanctions-resilience is described as a documented motive, not an endorsement; the same bypass rails carry the same state-control concerns as Western CBDCs.
A 'BRICS currency' is recorded as aspirational rhetoric, not an existing instrument.
These edges are OVERLAY/context and are EXCLUDED from the SCC / Z3 / TLA+ proofs.

edges.raw_instrument: bypasses SWIFT for cross-border CBDC settlement - the dollar-bypass alternative to the incumbent rail
USD-stablecoin dollar-extension rail as the private counter to the state bypass stack (defends dollar primacy)
edges.note: OVERLAY: dollar-extension vs bypass; excluded from proofs

limits: Maps the cross-border settlement contest: the state dollar-BYPASS stack (CIPS, Project mBridge live-2025-bypassing-SWIFT with ~95% e-CNY and Saudi/UAE membership, e-CNY, BRICS Pay) vs the private dollar-EXTENSION rail (USD stablecoins + SWIFT), with the reality-check that the dollar is still on 89.2% of FX even as its reserve share fell to ~54%. De-dollarization is real at the margins, not the core. Interleaves the stablecoin-Treasury, market-plumbing, Gulf-sovereign, and Russia-energy threads; the US stablecoin push reads partly as a dollar-defense counter-move. Overlay edges excluded from the formal proofs. source: see verification_log and the rendered .md for full URLs.
